REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 2 Igniting Transformation POWERING OUR CLIENTS' SUCCESS WITH AI In today's dynamic business environment, Artificial Intelligence is a powerful force, a significant disruptor with the potential to fundamentally reshape industries and operations. At Wipro, we take an AI-first approach to help clients transform. Our strategic imperative, an AIpowered Wipro, is designed with a clear, client-centric objective: to change the game for our client's businesses, deliver better services with enhanced productivity, and operate our own business better using AI. Our strategy focuses on three key pillars: Change the Game VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT AI-powered Wipro, Transforming the Future, Responsibly At Wipro, we believe that innovation and trust can go hand in hand. We know that business success depends on effective utilization of AI, and this is why we proactively tackle the complexities of this technology, emphasizing risk management and stringent governance practices. Enduring and sustainable innovation is built on a solid governance framework, and we collaborate closely with our clients and stakeholders to integrate governance, privacy, security, and legal

stringent governance practices. Enduring and sustainable innovation is built on a solid governance framework, and we collaborate closely with our clients and stakeholders to integrate governance, privacy, security, and legal protections into our innovations, empowering them to unlock the full potential of AI. Building a Better World The belief that our purpose fuels our business and our business fuels our purpose has been our guiding force since the very beginning. We commit ourselves to be a catalyst in building a just, equitable, humane, and sustainable society. Economic interest of about 2/3rd ownership of Wipro Limited is irrevocably earmarked to Azim Premji Foundation, the philanthropic organization STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 3 founded by Mr. Azim Premji. We care deeply about facilitating actionable transformation for our clients, our communities and the environment. As one of the first seven companies worldwide to receive SBTi-approved Net Zero targets, our commitment is deep and authentic. REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 14 Wipro Gen AI ALL SET TO REALIZE THE PROMISE OF AGENTIC AI AI is now delivering value to our customers and we are integrating AI across the ecosystem at a quicker pace. We are rapidly moving from pilots to production and Wipro is enabling this transformation at speed with the ‘Change the Game’ strategy. We enable this through a three-pronged strategy of transforming business with: Process Transformation, with Wipro’s AI-powered Industry and Cross-Industry solutions AI CoE model to adopt AI innovations faster with Responsible AI foundation Technology solutions to accelerate the adoption We continue to invest in WeGA (Wipro Enterprise Generative AI) studio, helping customers adopt GenAI in a faster, responsible and cost-effective manner. Leveraging ‘Wipro AI Platform’ for SDLC, we are helping our customers to reduce the ‘time to market’ on transformation engagements and improving system availability in managed services. Agentic AI ‘Human agency’ refers to the capacity of humans to interpret their surroundings and take deliberate actions. Agentic AI represents a significant advancement, enabling AI to possess

managed services. Agentic AI ‘Human agency’ refers to the capacity of humans to interpret their surroundings and take deliberate actions. Agentic AI represents a significant advancement, enabling AI to possess agency. This shift is transforming Human-AI collaboration from AI-assisted humans to autonomous agents managed by humans, with AI Copilots being gradually replaced by Autonomous AI Agents. Multimodal Agentic Systems are expected to simplify user interfaces and enhance customer experiences. AI agents could replace many applications with their innate advantages of context awareness, automated decision-making and the ability to take logical actions. Agentic systems will, therefore, help to reimagine business processes. However, currently there are challenges around reliability and consistency. These challenges can be addressed with algorithm enhancements for better controllability and planning. We have established an Agentic AI Acceleration Pod to assist clients with key transformational decisions, mitigate risks associated with adoption, and support the development of policies for responsible AI deployment. this transformative technology to benefit clients, with a proactive approach to mitigate associated risks. Wealth AI Reaching out to the younger generation of mass affluents, who are the future HNIs is the biggest challenge for wealth management firms. The FA (Financial Advisors) have to now deal with new generation of HNIs and provide personalized experience. This requires processing and inferencing of unstructured information from a multitude of sources, both internal and external. Wealth AI uses the classic ML (predictive) and Gen AI to tap into signals emerging from the collection of information to provide advice and build products that cater to emerging needs of HNIs. impact, manufacturing quality and workplace safety in Energy, Utilities and Manufacturing sectors. We have also expanded the use of Inspect AI into banking, financial services and consumer industries. E-IQ (Enterprise Intelligence Quotient – Our Agentic AI rollout methodology) At Wipro, we help our client measure and scale AI adoption across technology and business processes. At the process level, we benchmark intelligence of a business process and recommend Agentic AI use cases, roll out roadmap and technology architecture to improve the intelligence quotient and ROI in a measurable way. The use cases, when implemented, improve the E-IQ. Our templatized AI CoE (Center of Excellence) rolls out agent catalogues across the enterprise for implementation. We launched Sovereign AI, in collaboration with NVIDIA, to help individual nations around the world with their capabilities to produce artificial intelligence using their own infrastructure, data,

enterprise for implementation. We launched Sovereign AI, in collaboration with NVIDIA, to help individual nations around the world with their capabilities to produce artificial intelligence using their own infrastructure, data, workforce and business networks. The partnership, which is part of Wipro’s ai360 strategy, brings together Wipro’s locally governed AI frameworks powered by Wipro’s WeGA Studio, NVIDIA’s AI Enterprise software and Meta’s Llama to enable: • Privacy and security compliance to ensure data and AI sovereignty • Customization of Large Language Models for local language and domain needs • Access to pre-built responsible AI accelerators to help clients rapidly build customized use cases and high-performance models that support their unique objectives Integrated Annual Report 2024-25

Wipro produced a carbon reduction roadmap with the steps to meet its 2030 50% reduction target and set them up on a path to achieve their 100% reduction target by 2050. JFKIAT implemented the recommended renewable energy sourcing strategy which allowed them to reduce their baseline emissions by 16% in 2024. REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK Vorwerk Selects Wipro to Manage and Transform their IT Ecosystem Wipro was selected by Vorwerk, the number one direct sales company in Europe and a global seller of highquality household appliances, to manage and transform its IT landscape. This five-year engagement will futureproof Vorwerk's IT infrastructure by leveraging Wipro's AI-powered infrastructure operations solution. Wipro will consolidate Vorwerk’s business applications, infrastructure, and cybersecurity tracks onto a unified monitoring platform, providing better visibility into the company’s technology ecosystem and driving increased operational efficiencies. Wipro will also build an end-toend support portal to rapidly and seamlessly enhance the end-user experience. REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 28 CHAIRMAN’S LETTER TO STAKEHOLDERS Dear Stakeholders, I hope this letter finds you well. FY25 was a year of significant change for Wipro and the world. As Srini stepped into his role as CEO & MD, we navigated an ever-changing external environment shaped by evolving tariffs, geopolitical tensions, and rapid technological advancements like AI. Generative AI became central to business strategy, enhancing productivity, reimagining processes, unlocking growth, and creating meaningful experiences. We focused on staying relevant to clients, executing with discipline, and making bold, future-ready investments. These choices are shaping Wipro’s next chapter. In FY25, we reported $10.5 billion in revenue, a 2.3% year-on-year decrease in constant currency. Net income stood at H131.4 billion, growing 18.9% YoY. IT services operating margin was 17.1%, expanding 0.9% over FY24. Earnings per share were H12.6, a 20.3% increase YoY. Operating cash flow was close to $2.0 billion, at 128% of net income. Effective FY26, we have revised our minimum committed payout to 70% of net income, calculated cumulatively over three years. A 1:1

close to $2.0 billion, at 128% of net income. Effective FY26, we have revised our minimum committed payout to 70% of net income, calculated cumulatively over three years. A 1:1 bonus issue in Q2 and an interim dividend of H6 per share in Q3 underscore our commitment to enhanced shareholder returns. This December, Wipro turns 80—a rare milestone in any industry. For nearly 50 years, we have been in the technology business—navigating disruption through continuous innovation, deep client trust, an adaptable culture, and a long-term mindset. Our innovation strategy is now anchored by the Wipro Innovation Network—a global ecosystem integrating our innovation labs, Wipro Ventures, partner labs, cloud studios, and the Topcoder community. This network accelerates co-creation and delivers next-generation solutions at scale.

VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT Wipro Ventures, which completed ten years this February, remains a pillar of our innovation journey. Launched in 2015, it connects enterprise clients to the global startup ecosystem. Now, a $500 million fund, it backs early to mid-stage companies building innovative enterprise software, many focused on AI and automation. Wipro helps these startups scale, bringing new ideas to market and driving strategic value for clients. We are sharpening our portfolio with a strategic focus on consulting and AI. The Capco acquisition laid a strong foundation in financial services consulting, which we’ve since scaled across industries. Our AI investments are powering transformation across client engagements and internal operations. Agentic AI is delivering impact across HR, finance, legal, and more. Our internal medical claims solution processes over 10,000 claims monthly, completely touchless, enhancing both speed and service quality. We are building an AI-first workforce. Nearly all employees have completed foundational GenAI training, and over 87,000 have received advanced, role-specific upskilling. Through Talent@Scale, we have partnered with leading academic institutions to establish Centres of Excellence providing industry-aligned training

have completed foundational GenAI training, and over 87,000 have received advanced, role-specific upskilling. Through Talent@Scale, we have partnered with leading academic institutions to establish Centres of Excellence providing industry-aligned training in high-demand skills. These moves reflect our commitment to long-term value creation and deeper client partnerships. Guided by our Values We remain committed to building an inclusive and supportive workplace where everyone feels they belong. This year, we deepened our focus on employee well-being and self-care—helping individuals thrive both personally and professionally. Our approach is flexible and thoughtful, designed to meet people where they are in their journey. Recognizing that well-being isn’t one-size-fits-all, we have built a framework offering customized support— covering physical health, mental well-being, and work-life balance. Our goal is to ensure every associate feels empowered, supported, valued, and equipped to be their best. hire and grow—37% of our workforce is women, 37% are Gen Z, and we continue to build a multigenerational, diverse workplace that values gender, ethnicity, orientation, ability, and more. As I reflect on these twelve months, I see meaningful progress and clear opportunities for improvement. Navigating a Shifting Environment Macroeconomic uncertainty persisted throughout the year, deepening in the fourth quarter due to global headwinds like tariff increases. This impacted business sentiment, particularly in consumer and manufacturing sectors, with some clients delaying or adjusting transformation programs. Yet, the demand for technology-led reinvention remained strong. Clients sought AI-powered solutions that deliver speed, scale, and sustained value. Wipro continued to support this shift, serving as a trusted transformation partner. Our IT Services revenue for FY25 was $10.5 billion, a 2.3% decline in constant currency. Growth was primarily driven by enterprises prioritizing operational excellence in a tough macroeconomic environment. With stabilizing inflation, a cooling labor market and interest rate cuts, technology spending is expected to improve. Enterprises are expected to focus on scaled adoption of AI with outcome-driven use cases and are likely to invest in technology refresh as a precursor to the same. Global IT service providers are equipped to support enterprises across industries with a wide range of consulting services, business process services, technology services across AI and GenAI-based offerings, digital transformation, cloud, application development, maintenance and support, data and analytics, cybersecurity and engineering and R&D. IT services industry to play a pivotal role in supporting enterprises across key areas such as AI and GenAI experimentation and scaled adoption, cost optimization, operational excellence, digital transformation, security advisory and managed services, vendor consolidation, customer experience, innovation in products and services and talent strategies.

as a percentage of IT Services revenue from 11.32% for the year ended March 31, 2024 to 11.07% for the year ended March 31, 2025, and optimization of overheads such as travel and communication expenses during fiscal year 2025. We continually monitor our funding requirements and strategies are executed to maintain sufficient flexibility to access global funding sources, as needed. As of March 31, 2025, we had contractual commitments of H8,719 million ($102.06 million) related to capital expenditures on the construction or expansion of software development facilities and H40,264 million ($471.31 million) related to other purchase obligations. Plans to construct or expand our software development facilities are determined by our business requirements. As discussed above, cash generated from operations is our primary source of liquidity. We believe that our cash and cash equivalents along with cash generated from operations will be sufficient to meet our working capital requirements as well as repayment obligations with respect to debt and borrowings. REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 46 ESG STRATEGY AND GOVERNANCE At Wipro, our ESG and Sustainability program resonates our core values, guided by global industry benchmarks and standards, stakeholder feedback and materiality assessments. The Spirit of Wipro encompasses responsibility, respect for individuals and integrity. We firmly believe that sustainability is a collective responsibility. This commitment extends horizontally across all levels, functions and business units, with numerous departments being pivotal to its success. Our Global Operations team, Global People Function, Investor Relations team and Legal team are instrumental in anchoring, defining and implementing the identified ESG goals. Furthermore, oversight of these sustainability programs is maintained at the corporate level, under the supervision of our Chairman, Board of Governors and Executive Board. Governance The overarching responsibility for the Company's ESG charter rests with the Chief Executive Officer (CEO). This includes Climate Change as a key element. The Chief Sustainability Officer (CSO)* reports to the CEO and is in charge of the overall sustainability charter. The CSO is also part of the Wipro Executive Board, the senior-most executive

Chief Sustainability Officer (CSO)* reports to the CEO and is in charge of the overall sustainability charter. The CSO is also part of the Wipro Executive Board, the senior-most executive body within the organization, comprising a core group of corporate leaders. Our Board’s governance of sustainability falls under the Nomination and Remuneration Committee (that also serves as the Sustainability Committee) and is led by an Independent Director. This apex body oversees Wipro's sustainability policy and programs. Composed entirely of Independent Directors, the Committee also reviews the Company's policies on Corporate Social Responsibility, including public issues of significance to the Company and its stakeholders. * The present Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO) also assumes the responsibilities of the Chief Sustainability Officer (CSO). At Wipro, all sustainability programs adhere to the organization’s regular planning and budgeting cycles. These programs are reviewed quarterly at multiple levels, by the board, the Group Executive Council and the Chairman. The progress of ESG goals is monitored by the Board during quarterly meetings. REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 52 MATERIALIT Y ASSESSMENT Strategic stakeholder engagement allows us to identify and prioritize the most significant issues for our stakeholders. This process, also referred to as materiality determination, helps us craft potential outcomes that maximize value creation and positive impact for our stakeholders. Materiality assessment is the process of identifying and evaluating the most relevant issues that could impact either our organization or our stakeholders or both. Through intensive stakeholder consultations, we strive to understand the relative importance of these concerns. It enables us to prioritize list of issues that help to focus resources, funding and management attention in the process. It allows us to align our goals and targets with the organization’s strategy. Identification of key material topics also supports the analysis and mitigation of risks, empowering us to leverage new opportunities and earn the trust of stakeholders. Our Approach to Determining Material Issues: 1 Identify 2 Design 3 4 • Prioritize Issues Analyze Double Materiality Double materiality is a concept that recognizes the dual perspective of materiality: the

Approach to Determining Material Issues: 1 Identify 2 Design 3 4 • Prioritize Issues Analyze Double Materiality Double materiality is a concept that recognizes the dual perspective of materiality: the impact of ESG issues on the Company and the impact of the Company on our stakeholders. This approach ensures that we consider both financial materiality (how ESG issues affect the Company's financial performance) and impact materiality (how the Company's activities affect the environment and society). While Wipro did a preliminary version of the Double Materiality Assessment (DMA) as part of our overall Materiality exercise in 2023, we intend to do a more comprehensive exercise in FY26. Designing a methodology for stakeholder engagement that includes the identification of a representative set of internal and external stakeholders and the creation of customized questionnaires to capture stakeholders’ responses. Data collection from internal and external stakeholders using surveys, along with FGDs for selecting stakeholders. REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 56 IMPACT VALUATION REPORT Impact valuation involves quantifying and appraising a company's positive and negative externalities within the context of society and the environment. While the underlying methodologies are still evolving and are open to debate, it serves as a useful barometer for identifying value additive and subtractive areas in the value chain. The objective of impact valuation, therefore, is to identify both the intended and unintended effects of business activities across products and services, operations, and the supply chain. By systematically and comprehensively accounting for these externalities, businesses can make decisions that create value for all stakeholders, not just shareholders. Since 2016, we have been calculating our Environmental Profit & Loss (EP&L). Also referred to as Natural Capital Valuation, this exercise helps us assign a financial value to our natural capital. Over the last two years, we have also been computing our Human Capital Valuation, which assesses the financial value of our impacts on our employees and our Produced Capital Valuation, which evaluates the financial value created for different

been computing our Human Capital Valuation, which assesses the financial value of our impacts on our employees and our Produced Capital Valuation, which evaluates the financial value created for different stakeholders. Wipro’s Natural Capital Valuation Program Assessment of natural capital impacts involve the use of methodologies that help companies discover their hitherto hidden impacts (both positive and negative) on nature or natural capital. Discovering hidden impacts helps companies in informed decision-making and identifying future risks and opportunities. It is a rigorous framework that assesses and quantifies impacts across the six key performance indicators (KPIs) that are part of Environmental factors. The methodology uses a value for the social cost of carbon that varies with country, sector and geography. Typically, it uses a higher discount rate for developing countries as compared to developed countries, as the former needs more ‘ecological space’ and ‘time’ to fulfil their developmental imperatives. Methodology To calculate the impacts of air pollution, only human health impacts were considered as they contribute to 95% of the total impact. Land use valuation is based on net change in economic

the impacts of air pollution, only human health impacts were considered as they contribute to 95% of the total impact. Land use valuation is based on net change in economic value due to loss of ecosystem services. In our case, it is calculated only for the electricity procured from the grid mix because for direct operations, land use change is not considered to be material. For calculating the impact of water consumption, the impact on human health, including the incidence of infectious diseases, and the impact of energy consumption were considered. Factors Considered for Impact Accounting Environmental Parameters Social Parameters Includes GHG emissions, air pollution, water consumption, waste generation, water and land pollution and land use change. For the reporting year, this exercise was done for our Company as well as our suppliers (Purchased Goods and Services). Includes labor practices, community engagement, human rights and health and safety. Economic Parameters Includes financial performance, economic impacts and value chain analysis.

VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS Our NCV for FY25 The overall impacts can be broken down into two components – Operational Impacts and Upstream Impacts. In FY25, total environmental costs of Wipro’s operations and upstream were quantified at $0.35 billion ($0.27 billion in FY24), of which operational and upstream impacts contribute 2% ($7 million) and 98% ($343 million), respectively. The operational impacts have reduced this year, primarily due to the transition to renewable energy. The contribution from electricity decreased substantially by 26% ($5 million to $3.7 million). However, there has been an increase in valuation from our upstream activities. This is primarily due to employees returning to office, aligned with work-from-office requirements. Within Wipro’s upstream supply chain, purchased goods and services across all tiers of suppliers (90%; $310 million) and upstream leased assets as well as upstream fuel and energy-related activities (at 3.5% each; $12 million each) are the top impact categories. In terms of the sources of impact, air pollution (71%; $250 million) and GHG emissions (18%; $62 million) are the top two

$12 million each) are the top impact categories. In terms of the sources of impact, air pollution (71%; $250 million) and GHG emissions (18%; $62 million) are the top two contributors. GHG emissions in Wipro’s operations 57 have seen an overall decline due to the transition to renewable energy. The source of air pollution is primarily from business travel and employee commute. The air pollution impact from business travel has risen since 2024 largely due to increase in domestic business travel by surface, air and offshore conveyance. The work-fromoffice requirements also resulted in increased air pollution due to employee commute. FY25 saw a visible increase in our natural capital impact valuation. Some of the key reasons for this are listed below: • The increased work-from-office percentage (up to 90%) across all our offices worldwide. As a result, the employee commute emissions – both GHG as well as air emissions – saw a significant ramp-up. • Increased occupancy also resulted in an increase in waste generation and water consumption, with its associated impacts. • While the total energy projected to be

saw a significant ramp-up. • Increased occupancy also resulted in an increase in waste generation and water consumption, with its associated impacts. • While the total energy projected to be consumed at upstream leased sites has reduced in 2025, there has been a rise in impacts largely due to an increase in electricity consumption at US leased sites. Assessing the Natural Capital as a measure of EBIT over the last three years, we find that this intensity metric has decreased across all parameters. This reduction of our impact intensities demonstrates our commitment to reduce the impact of our operations on the Natural Capital. Waste generation has increased primarily due to an increase in C&D waste. Sustainability, Climate Change and Technology Tackling climate change remains one of the most urgent and complex challenges facing humanity. The year 2024 was the hottest year on record and the first to breach 1.5°C, the climate change limit set by the Paris Agreement in 2015. Estimates suggest the technology sector contributes up to 4% of global carbon emissions, primarily from the consumption of electricity – comparable overall to that of the aviation industry. Projections indicate that this could reach 14% in the next few decades as demand for technology and AI increases, with data center power demand expected to grow 160% by 2030. The entire industry must reduce carbon emissions from a 2020 baseline by 45% in 2030 if we are to limit global warming to 1.5°C. VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 69 Program Highlights The WISE Program: A Comprehensive Overview The Wipro Initiative for Supplier Engagement (WISE) is a strategic program managed by Wipro’s Global Procurement team in collaboration with the Corporate Sustainability team. It is designed to support suppliers— particularly micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs)—who may not yet have their own sustainability frameworks. The program aims to build long-term, collaborative relationships with these suppliers by helping them develop and implement sustainability practices aligned with Wipro’s broader ESG goals. Through WISE, suppliers are guided to establish effective policies and processes that contribute to Net Zero carbon emissions, water conservation and waste reduction. The initiative also emphasizes social responsibility and ethical governance practices. Suppliers are selected for the program based on longterm, consistent business engagement and limited existing sustainability capabilities. The engagement begins with an ESG maturity assessment via a structured questionnaire. Based on the results, Wipro provides data-driven guidance and mentorship to help suppliers build comprehensive ESG programs. This includes multiple sessions of joint work to assess environmental impacts

structured questionnaire. Based on the results, Wipro provides data-driven guidance and mentorship to help suppliers build comprehensive ESG programs. This includes multiple sessions of joint work to assess environmental impacts and develop tailored sustainability strategies. Overall, WISE reflects Wipro’s commitment to fostering a responsible and resilient WISE Wipro Initiative for Supplier Engagement Environment Governance Social supply chain. By empowering suppliers with the tools and knowledge to operate sustainably, the program not only supports Wipro’s ESG objectives but also contributes to a more sustainable global ecosystem. VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 71 Risks and Opportunities Risk Opportunities Wipro’s dependence on our global supply chain may pose a significant risk in case of any disruption, whether it is geopolitical or climate. The inability of our Suppliers to deliver services or products could result in reputational damage, contractual non-compliance and financial risks. Wipro undertakes risk assessments to identify potential supplier-related risks and it is actively managed through professional methodologies. In addition, ensuring compliance with Environmental, Social, Governance (ESG) criteria presents another substantial organizational challenge to Wipro. Wipro has a comprehensive Third-Party Risk Management Framework, that governs the lifecycle. Our onboarding checks involve technical and financial assessments and enhanced due diligences and supplier risk assessments basis the risk profile of the Supplier. Our Third-Party risk management framework evaluates the risks associated with third parties; including supplier concentration, category wise spends, contractual status, documentation, etc. We tapped this opportunity to engage with our suppliers on various aspects of ESG through the WISE program. This enhances our business resilience and prepares us for the upcoming

etc. We tapped this opportunity to engage with our suppliers on various aspects of ESG through the WISE program. This enhances our business resilience and prepares us for the upcoming disclosure landscape. Further, data analytics tools enable organizations to conduct deeper performance assessments of their suppliers' ESG activities and monitor sustainability improvements properly. The integration of AI technology enhances the capability to do risk assessments through predictions of supply chain disruptions and recognition of suppliers who represent high risk. VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 75 Overview of Social and Community Initiatives Note: Figures in this section are specific to FY25. Education: A Key Enabler of Community Well-Being Our work in education is driven by the belief that education is a key enabler of social progress. We support long-term engagements that address systemic issues in school and college education. At the same time, we also focus on more immediate concerns such as school infrastructure and enabling access to education for socially disadvantaged sections. Children with Disabilities (CwD) and Sustainability Education are areas of particular interest and concern for us. The impacts of our work in education are both direct, and in many ways indirect, with second-order effects manifesting over a relatively long period. In FY25, our efforts to improve the quality of education, particularly in early childhood and primary education, reached 22 states and union territories, in collaboration with 86 partners. This includes 21 partners in the Wipro Education Fellowship Program, which supports earlystage education organizations focused on systemic change in India's school education

union territories, in collaboration with 86 partners. This includes 21 partners in the Wipro Education Fellowship Program, which supports earlystage education organizations focused on systemic change in India's school education system. We partnered with 47 organizations across 12 states and 2 union territories to enhance educational access for around 70,000 students from underserved communities. This includes the 'Urban Government School Initiative’, which aims to improve infrastructure and teaching practices, and develop model government schools. Our engagement with partners focusing on education for Children with Disabilities (CwD) has grown. We now work with 26 partners across 12 states and 2 union territories. In the process, we are reaching 68,068 CwD. Integrated Annual Report 2024-25

TalentNext, launched in 2016, aims to improve the quality of engineering education in India. Its goal is to bridge the gap between demand and supply of skilled professionals. Since FY21, we have covered 533 engineering and 113 science colleges from across India. We are committed to improve access to quality healthcare services to marginalized communities. Our approach aims to introduce lasting change that creates impact over the long-term. It complements the public health system, and systemically addresses under-served issues. This is achieved by augmenting the public health system, improving accessibility of healthcare services, building the capacity of local communities to manage their healthcare needs, and training health workers to address community needs. United States Started in 2012, Wipro Science Education Fellowship Program USA (Wipro SEF USA) is a two-year program designed to improve individual teacher practice, foster teacher leadership opportunities, and create a district corps of teacher leaders who can support sustainable positive changes in science education. Wipro SEF USA runs in partnership with seven universities. It

teacher leadership opportunities, and create a district corps of teacher leaders who can support sustainable positive changes in science education. United Kingdom Wipro SEF UK was launched in FY19, in partnership with King’s College, London (KCL), and Sheffield Hallam University (SHU). The program at KCL is the UK’s first Masters in STEM Education Program. It offers up to 15 bursaries per year to in-service and early-career STEM teachers. We strive to focus on the Wipro Teacher Fellowship and Wipro Teacher Mentor Program through our partnership with SHU. Through this initiative, we have supported over 100 fellows, mentors and senior mentors over the years. Wipro partners with healthcare non-profits to empower local communities to self-manage their healthcare needs. We supported 21 projects and 15 partners, including three projects on climate resilience interventions. The Urban Ecology Program is active in eight cities and one town. The Bengaluru Sustainability Forum (BSF), co-convened by Wipro Foundation, fosters interdisciplinary collaborations on urban sustainability. It also supports action-oriented projects through a small grants program. In FY25, six grants were awarded across Bengaluru and Mysuru, under this partnership. Under the community ecology initiative, Wipro supported 15,600+ people in the informal waste sector in Mysuru with training and social security. These saw participation from over 480 CSO (Civil Society Organization) representatives. The Education Partners’ Forum focused on 'Capacity building of CSOs'. It explored approaches to enhance the educational, organizational and operational capacities of CSOs to facilitate positive social change. The Healthcare Partners’ Forum aimed at 'Strengthening India’s public healthcare system'. It focused on public-private partnerships, and community mental health and well-being. VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 81 Urban Public Spaces Public spaces are crucial for fostering social integrity, inclusion, democracy and empathy. In FY25, we supported Pune International Centre (PIC), as part of the ‘Cities & Public Spaces’ Program. PIC is a Pune-based, leading think tank and policy research organization. It is dedicated to tackling complex global issues through comprehensive research, insightful dialogue and strategic policy recommendations. PIC organizes knowledge events, undertakes publications, supports the arts, offers scholarships, and promotes diverse cultural and socio-economic studies. The two other major projects we have been supporting, which act as inclusive public spaces, are Bangalore International Centre (BIC), and Museum of Art and Photography (MAP). BIC has rapidly become a vibrant hub for diverse conversations and cultural events. In FY25, MAP facilitated sessions for 2,119 students from across 33 schools. Both BIC and MAP are accessible for the public, free of cost. For nearly two decades now, we have adopted the ISO 14001:2015 standards to guide our Environmental Management System (EMS). REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK With nearly 84% of our campuses powered by renewable energy and our focus on reducing absolute water consumption year on year, we are on track to contribute to a cleaner future. We were among the pioneers of Green Building Design, with 24 of our buildings across campuses being LEED certified (Silver, Gold and Platinum) at commissioning. We continue to uphold these standards in facility maintenance as well. ENERGY Energy is a vital component of our operations and significantly contributes to our Scope 2 emissions. Demand for energy within our operational areas primarily arise from air conditioning, lighting and allied electrical systems. We are working on a two-pronged approach to reduce our energy footprint: energy efficiency and energy transition. While energy efficiency aims to reduce energy consumption by improving performance and outcomes, energy transition refers to our emphasis on consistent renewable energy adoption to reduce our carbon footprint. The sourcing model of renewable energy varies according to the state, but it largely consists of Private Purchase Agreements (PPA), Green Tariff schemes of state utilities and Group Captive. The Group Captive agreement has greatly accelerated our RE footprint across 4 states. Program Highlights Energy Efficiency Energy efficiency remains a core focus at Wipro. Through our architectural design and the use of energy-efficient equipment, we strive to enhance energy conservation initiatives. Moreover, to prevent unnecessary usage of energy, only occupied areas are cooled. We continuously aim to reduce energy intensity and improve efficiency in our physical

to enhance energy conservation initiatives. Moreover, to prevent unnecessary usage of energy, only occupied areas are cooled. We continuously aim to reduce energy intensity and improve efficiency in our physical and digital infrastructure. Our latest facilities in Bengaluru and Hyderabad are benchmarked against global best practices for energy efficient systems. Designed with a team of more than 50 engineers, architects and experts in building design, these campuses are built keeping in mind the natural elements and have features such as integrated underfloor air distribution (UFAD) system and rotary UPS to achieve an EPI of 65 units/sq.m, at full occupancy. Our campus in Kodathi houses the Global Energy Command Center (GECC) that monitors energy efficiency across 90% of our owned square footage in the country. Real time data from the GECC have enabled us to implement energy efficiency measures in our older campuses. These include: • Modernizing our Chiller and Air Handling Units (AHUs) • Optimizing UPS systems • Optimizing the number of blowers required Global Energy Command Center (GECC) Our GECC platform integrates Building Management System (BMS) inputs to optimize

Handling Units (AHUs) • Optimizing UPS systems • Optimizing the number of blowers required Global Energy Command Center (GECC) Our GECC platform integrates Building Management System (BMS) inputs to optimize operational control and enhance energy efficiency. It connects to IoTenabled devices and sensors across India, leveraging data analytics to ensure systems operate according to manufacturer efficiency curves. Any deviations are tracked and corrected with in-house or OEM support, and key Annual Maintenance Contracts (AMCs) are tied to energy efficiency and system availability outcomes. Across India, approximately, 17.33 million sq ft across India are connected to the BMS, covering 90% of our total office space. Since FY18, we have saved 4.57 million units of electricity annually, resulting in net savings of H37.6 million per annum.

VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT Our commitment to energy efficiency is also reflected through the adoption of the ISO 50001 Energy Management System (EnMS) across our campuses. In August 2022, three campuses (Kodathi, Chennai and Sarjapur 2) received ISO 50001 certifications, accounting for 35% of our total operational office space. Energy Transition Our energy transition journey started in 2018. Currently, 84% of our energy is sourced from renewable energy. While our renewable energy sourcing model varies from one state to the other, it primarily involves Private Purchase Agreements (PPA), Group Captive and Green Tariff Schemes of state utilities. Our investment in Group Captive two years ago has significantly accelerated renewable energy adoption. We have worked closely with RE developers, wherever possible, to ensure a just transition. Through our participation in the Renewable Energy Initiative (REI) led by the Forum for the Future, we aim to raise awareness about the social impacts of establishing RE plants and promote business models and value chains centered on justice, equity, universal rights and resilient ecological systems. STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 85 GREENHOUSE GAS (GHG) EMISSIONS Our sustainability strategy lays great emphasis on minimizing GHG emissions. Being one of the first seven companies worldwide to have SBTi-approved targets, Wipro has defined a clear Net Zero roadmap. Using the methodology in the GHG protocol to calculate and monitor our Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions, in FY25 we reported a reduction of 4% GHG emissions compared to last year. To achieve our goals, we are primarily relying on decarbonization, with a two-pronged approach – transition to renewable energy and increased energy efficiency. This is especially crucial for reducing our Scope 1 and 2 emissions. With improved methods for measurement of Scope 3 baseline emissions, we are establishing processes to mitigate them. With ambitious targets and investments in innovative technologies, we aim to reduce our carbon footprint and secure the foundation of a sustainable future. VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT Business Travel Wipro has been measuring emissions from business travel for several years. Business travel forms the third largest category of our Scope 3 emissions after Employee Commute and Purchased Goods and Services (accounting for around 16%). Over the last year, we have gained more granular knowledge on the highest sources of business travel emissions within the organization and are ready to launch a Sustainable Travel Policy. Our travel policy has integrated the use of alternative travel modes such as trains and buses, particularly in specific regions in India and the EU. Technology is also being used to prompt employees to adopt better travel choices. We are also working with top delivery accounts to promote voluntary low-carbon travel choices. Creating employee awareness forms an integral part of implementing this policy. Employee Commute GHG emissions from employee commutes increased considerably as people started to return to work. Accounting for 39% of our total Scope 3 emissions, we remain committed to adopt a conscious approach to limit our carbon footprint. As one of the first major

to work. Accounting for 39% of our total Scope 3 emissions, we remain committed to adopt a conscious approach to limit our carbon footprint. As one of the first major Indian companies to join the EV100 group, we are committed to making environmentally sound choices such as promoting the use of buses, trains and carpooling. The EV100 group, an initiative by the Climate Group, advocates electric mobility. We currently have formal EV contracts in Bengaluru, Hyderabad and Kochi, with a goal to transition our entire global fleet (excluding STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 87 employee-owned vehicles) to electric vehicles by 2030. Our Kochi campus has achieved 100% EV deployment, with other cities following suite. While 66% of our total commuted distance by company owned fleet is by EV and CNG, employee private transport accounts for a significant portion of our emissions. To address this, we exploring initiatives such as incentives to employees and partnering with public transport providers. we send less than 1% of untreated water (which is the reject water from our nanofiltration process) to an external vendor to process. Program Highlights Water Consumption We obtain our water from private water, municipal water in-situ groundwater and third-party purchases to meet our demands. Freshwater is procured from private providers, groundwater and municipalities, sourced primarily through groundwater extraction. Municipal water, however, is largely sourced from river and lake systems. In-situ groundwater contributes nearly 4% to our overall freshwater usage in various Indian cities. Additionally, we purchase some amount of treated water to supplement our water consumption requirements. We also remain committed to rainwater harvesting initiatives to eliminate our dependence on conventional freshwater sources and nurture a more sustainable future. Water Use Intensity Water use intensity offers key metrics for year-onyear comparison, helping Wipro manage resources better by measuring it through revenue, seat count and occupancy. Water use is directly dependent on occupancy, making it a more relevant intensity metric.

VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 89 Rainwater Harvesting All our campuses are equipped with rainwater harvesting systems, which either store treated water for facility use or aid in groundwater recharge through recharge wells. In FY24, we launched a 2,000KL rainwater harvesting project at our Chennai campus. The rainwater harvesting systems at Kodathi and CDC5 feature significant storage capacities and unique designs. Kodathi has a 17,000 KL capacity, capturing rainwater from 24 acres of open space and landscape, with additional features like biodiversity landscaping and a walkway bridge. CDC5, with a 2,000 KL capacity, captures rainwater from 21,000 sqm of terrace space and utilizes the area above the tanks for car parking. Together, these systems demonstrate a comprehensive approach to rainwater harvesting, integrating significant storage capacities, extensive catchment areas, and unique features to enhance functionality and sustainability. ASSESSING NATURAL CAPITAL Natural Capital Valuation (NCV) is a comprehensive framework that evaluates the operations and value chain of a company for positive and negative impacts on Natural Capital and assigns a monetary value based on the social cost of the impacts. The evaluation is based on six key environmental KPIs that are a part of Environmental factors, using a social cost of carbon that varies from one place to the other. RISK MANAGEMENT VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS 109 STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS Climate and ESG Risks Strategy As a global technology services leader, we view the management of ESG and climate-related risks as fundamental to our long-term resilience and value creation. At Wipro, we continue to navigate an evolving climate landscape, addressing risks and capitalizing on opportunities to support the low-carbon transition. Climate change influences our operations, value chains, and financial performance, driving us to embed these considerations into our governance, strategy, and risk management processes. This section reflects our commitment to the IFRS S2 Climate-related Disclosures framework, offering a clear view of our approach. Our strategy embeds ESG and climate factors into business planning across short (1-3 years), medium (3-10 years), and long (beyond 10 years) term horizons, reflecting their critical role in our operations and stakeholder expectations. Risk Governance Our Board oversees Sustainability as well as ESG risks through the Board Committees. While the Nomination and Remuneration Committee oversees our Sustainability and CSR activities, the Audit, Risk, and Compliance Committee evaluates climate and ESG risks alongside enterprise concerns,

through the Board Committees. While the Nomination and Remuneration Committee oversees our Sustainability and CSR activities, the Audit, Risk, and Compliance Committee evaluates climate and ESG risks alongside enterprise concerns, ensuring strategic alignment. The sustainability team, reporting quarterly to the Chairman and Nomination and Remuneration Committee, drives execution. This structure helps embed climate considerations at the highest level, with oversight cascading through our Business Sustainability Office and functional groups such as Facilities Management Group, Human Resources and Global Procurement Group. City Water Stress RCP 4.5 RCP 8.5 Climate-related Risks and Scenario Analysis At Wipro, we proactively assess and manage climaterelated risks using scenario analysis with RCPs 4.5 and 8.5 to guide our strategies. Under RCP 4.5, eight cities where Wipro has operations are projected to experience water scarcity, while one city is expected to be severely impacted and six cities are expected to be moderately impacted by heatwaves. In the RCP 8.5 high-emission scenario, Wipro is likely to face extreme rainfall events and urban flooding in at least three of its locations. Timely assessment of climate risks allows us to

RCP 8.5 high-emission scenario, Wipro is likely to face extreme rainfall events and urban flooding in at least three of its locations. Timely assessment of climate risks allows us to integrate mitigation plans into our strategy. For example, in facilities that may face water scarcity, we have implemented rainwater harvesting in addition to procuring treated water for our operations. These initiatives are anticipated to minimize our freshwater intake. A summary of the results from the Climate Risk Assessment done 5 years ago is given below. A detailed report can be found here. REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK ESG-related Risks ESG risks are identified and assessed using a range of qualitative and quantitative methods, including a megatrend analysis, SWOT study, ESG materiality assessments, stress testing and a what-if scenario analysis. These risks are mapped to other enterprise risks, as well as controls, testing processes, compliance requirements, risk owners, reporting lines and strategic objectives combined together in a single integrated data model. The result is a holistic risk overview that empowers management to make better-informed decisions and provide better risk oversight. This holistic approach helps us address ESG challenges, seize opportunities and support low-carbon transition. Risk Management Our risk framework integrates ESG and climate risks into Enterprise Risk Management (ERM), aligned with COSO ERM framework, enhancing resilience and transparency across operations. The COSO ERM Framework defines ERM as ‘the culture, capabilities and practices, integrated with strategy-setting and performance, that organizations rely on to manage risk in creating, preserving and realizing value1’. Wipro’s objective in evolving ERM to include ESG follows COSO framework to achieve: Enhanced Resilience A common language for articulating ESG-related

to manage risk in creating, preserving and realizing value1’. Wipro’s objective in evolving ERM to include ESG follows COSO framework to achieve: Enhanced Resilience A common language for articulating ESG-related risks Improved resource deployment Enhanced pursuit of ESG-related opportunities Realized efficiencies of scale Improved disclosure ERM is intended to be ongoing and iterative, embedded in everyday business processes to allow the entity to stay aware and ahead of emerging threats and opportunities. SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 110 Integration with Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Our Climate and ESG risks are included and assessed as part of our Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Risk Register. Incorporating these risks into existing risk registers elevates the significance of ESG in senior management discussions and provides visibility as to how ESG risks influence and interact with other enterprise risks. This cross-functional mapping across functions such as Facilities Management, HR, Data Privacy and Business Resilience ensure appropriate investments and mitigation controls. Risk Identification, Assessment and Mitigation Our four-step process identifies, assesses and mitigates risks: • Identification: Proactive ESG and climate risks identification is done through stakeholder engagement, materiality

and mitigation controls. Risk Identification, Assessment and Mitigation Our four-step process identifies, assesses and mitigates risks: • Identification: Proactive ESG and climate risks identification is done through stakeholder engagement, materiality assessments, gap analyses and SWOT studies. • Assessment: Risks are assessed using scenario analysis and a risk matrix, evaluating severity, likelihood and financial impact across dimensions like safety, infrastructure and customer delivery. • Treatment: Developing and implementing tailored mitigation strategies for prioritized risks, supported by detailed business cases and management approval. • Reporting: Continuously monitored via Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) and reported internally to the Board and externally to stakeholders, ensuring compliance and transparency.

REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK Key Financial and Market Risks Taxation Risks Profits earned from providing services at client premises outside India may be subject to tax in the country where we perform the work. Most of the taxes paid in countries other than India can be applied as a credit against Indian tax liability to the extent that the same income is subject to taxation in India. Currently, we benefit from certain tax incentives under Indian tax laws. These tax incentives include a tax holiday from payment of Indian corporate income taxes for our businesses operating from designated SEZs. Wage Pressure Our wage costs in India have historically been significantly lower than wage costs in the U.S. and Europe for comparably skilled professionals, and this has been one of our competitive advantages. However, wage increases in India may prevent us from sustaining this competitive advantage and may negatively affect our profit margins. We have historically experienced significant competition for employees from large multinational companies that have established and continue to establish offshore operations in India, as well

negatively affect our profit margins. We have historically experienced significant competition for employees from large multinational companies that have established and continue to establish offshore operations in India, as well as from companies within India. We may need to increase the levels of our employee compensation more rapidly than in the past to retain talent. Once the effective date is notified by the Government of India, we may also experience increased costs in future years for employment and post-employment benefits in India as a result of the issuance of the Code on Social Security, 2020. Unless we are able to continue to increase the efficiency and productivity of our employees over the long term, wage increases may reduce our profit margins. Furthermore, any inability to increase the proportion of employees with less experience or source talent from other low-cost locations, like Eastern Europe, China or Southeast Asia could also negatively affect our profits. Credit Risk Credit risk arises from the possibility that customers may not be able to settle their obligations as agreed. To manage this, we periodically assess the

affect our profits. Credit Risk Credit risk arises from the possibility that customers may not be able to settle their obligations as agreed. To manage this, we periodically assess the credit rating SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 112 and financial reliability of customers, considering the financial condition, current economic trends, forwardlooking macroeconomic information, analysis of historical bad debts and aging of accounts receivable. No single customer accounted for more than 10% of the accounts receivable as of March 31, 2025 or revenues for the year ended March 31, 2025. There is no significant concentration of credit risk. Foreign Currency Risk We operate internationally and a major portion of our business is transacted in several currencies. Consequently, the Company is exposed to foreign exchange risk for receiving payment for sales and services provided in the United States and other regions. The Company is also exposed to foreign exchange risk for making purchases from overseas suppliers in various foreign currencies. The exchange rate risk primarily arises from foreign exchange revenue, receivables, cash balances, forecasted cash flows, payables and foreign currency loans and borrowings. A

overseas suppliers in various foreign currencies. The exchange rate risk primarily arises from foreign exchange revenue, receivables, cash balances, forecasted cash flows, payables and foreign currency loans and borrowings. A significant portion of our revenue is in U.S. Dollars, Pound Sterling, Euro, Australian Dollars and Canadian Dollars while a large portion of our costs are in Indian Rupees. The exchange rates between Rupee and these currencies have fluctuated significantly in recent years and may continue to fluctuate in the future. Appreciation of the Indian Rupee against these currencies can adversely affect our results of operations. Interest Rate Risk Interest rate risk primarily arises from floating rate investments and borrowings, including various revolving and other lines of credit. The Company’s investments are primarily in shortterm investments, which do not expose it to significant interest rate risk. From time to time, the Company manages its net exposure to interest rate risk relating to borrowings by entering into interest rate swap agreements, which allows it to exchange periodic payments based on a notional amount and agreed upon fixed and floating interest rates.

VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 113 Counterparty Risk Liquidity Risk Counterparty risk encompasses issuer risk on marketable securities, settlement risk on derivative and money market contracts and credit risk on cash and time deposits. Issuer risk is minimized by buying securities in India which are at least AA rated by Indian rating agencies. Settlement and credit risk is reduced by the policy of entering into transactions with counterparties that are usually banks or financial institutions with acceptable credit ratings. Exposure to these risks is closely monitored and maintained within predetermined parameters. There are limits on credit exposure to any financial institution. The limits are regularly assessed and determined based upon credit analysis including financial statements and capital adequacy ratio reviews. Liquidity risk is defined as the risk that we will not be able to settle or meet our obligations on time or at a reasonable price. Our corporate treasury department is responsible for liquidity and funding as well as settlement management. In addition, processes and policies related to such risks are overseen by senior

reasonable price. Our corporate treasury department is responsible for liquidity and funding as well as settlement management. In addition, processes and policies related to such risks are overseen by senior management. Management monitors the Company’s net liquidity position through rolling forecasts on the basis of expected cash flows. As of March 31, 2025, our cash and cash equivalents are held with major banks and financial institutions. Integrated Annual Report 2024-25

Position of the Company The business environment showcased resilience amidst the economic uncertainties in the financial year ended March 31, 2025. Despite a decline in global inflation, services inflation remained persistent, putting pressure on monetary policy normalization. Growth in advanced economies remained modest while developing economies experienced slightly higher growth rates, driven by declining inflation and increasing capital flows. Supply chains continued to face disruptions from geopolitical tensions. Enterprises adopted a cautiously optimistic outlook by investing in technologies to stay resilient and mitigate risks. The recent tariff announcements have resulted in heightened uncertainty and has impacted the demand environment in certain sectors. Considering this, we expect the clients to take a more measured approach on their IT spends and continue to focus on cost optimization, which should result in more cost-take out and vendor consolidation deals. Cyber Security Cybersecurity risk management is an integral part of your Company’s overall enterprise risk management program. Your Company’s cybersecurity risk management program is managed by its Chief Information Security Office function. Your Company’s cybersecurity risk management framework is defined and implemented to identify, assess, evaluate, treat, monitor, and report cyber risks across your IT infrastructure, applications, platforms, intellectual property, critical processes, technology solutions and third-party services. Cybersecurity risk assessment results and the status of the risk treatment plans are reviewed by management on a periodic basis. Your Company assesses and governs the cybersecurity program using selected industry best practices and frameworks from the International Organization for Standardization (“ISO”) and the National Institute of Standards and Technology (“NIST”).  With the rise of connected devices, the transition to cloud services, the adoption of AI, Generative AI and other emerging technologies, and other factors,

During the year, your Company supported nearly 225 projects in the domains of Education, Primary Health Care, Digital Skilling and Urban Ecology with an effective outreach and impact on nearly 4 Million people, a significant proportion of which was from vulnerable populations. d. Your Company’s work in education starts with early child education and engages with critical determinants of improvement in issues of quality and equity in schools. Your Company also supports thematic focus on STEM and Computer Science learning in schools, Digital skilling in colleges and Sustainability Education as a horizontal, crosscutting initiative. Your Company’s geographic presence in India spans 30 states where a network of more than 150 partners work on the ground creating positive outcomes for 2.7 Million students of which nearly 130,000 are children with disability. e. Your Company supports work in the crucial domain of improving the livability of our cities for the urban poor; our network of 15 field partners work

children with disability. e. Your Company supports work in the crucial domain of improving the livability of our cities for the urban poor; our network of 15 field partners work on 21 projects in 8 cities across India in the areas of water, climate adaptation, biodiversity and waste management. f. Based on the core idea of integrating wellbeing, personal purpose and social impact, your Company launched ‘Be the Change’, a program that has significantly expanded volunteering opportunities for employees. Since its launch, around 344 volunteering events have been organized which saw a significant increase in the participation of employees; 15,814 Wiproites across India and other geographies collectively spent nearly 35,000 hours in volunteering activities including blood donation, assembling science kits for schools and tree plantation. Variable Pay Compensation The variable pay of executive officers, including the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director, is based on clearly laid out criteria and measures, which are

the Company. Variable Pay Compensation The variable pay of executive officers, including the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director, is based on clearly laid out criteria and measures, which are linked to the desired performance and business objectives of the organization. The criteria for variable pay, which is paid out quarterly/annually, includes financial parameters like revenue, profit achievement, operating margin achievement and other strategic goals as decided by the Board, from time to time. Apart from the variable pay component, long term (typically greater than one year) incentives granted to executive officers, including the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director, includes both time-based stock units (RSUs) and performance- based stock units (PSUs). The vesting of PSUs is based on performance parameters of the Company over a defined performance period and is linked to predefined financial goals. Time-based stock units typically vest over a defined period. The vesting pattern and schedule for both these types of stock units are as determined by the Nomination and Remuneration Committee. VALUE CREATION FOR STAKEHOLDERS RISK MANAGEMENT STATUTORY REPORTS AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 147 Corporate Governance Report I. WIPRO’S PHILOSOPHY ON CORPORATE GOVERNANCE At Wipro, corporate governance is more than merely a compliance requirement, it is a core enabler of long‑term value creation. Our governance philosophy is deeply rooted in the Spirit of Wipro, which reflects our unwavering commitment to ethical conduct, respect for all stakeholders and a passion for excellence. These values are beyond just principles; they define who we are, how we operate and what we aspire to become. In early 2020, our Chairman introduced the Five Habits as part of a Company-wide culture transformation initiative aimed at fostering a growth mindset. These habits are designed to translate our enduring values, defined in the Spirit of Wipro into everyday behaviors that shape how we lead, collaborate and grow. Together, the Spirit of Wipro and the Five Habits reinforce our organizational identity and guide our conduct in a rapidly evolving business environment. Spirit of Wipro Five Habits These values are our bedrock. They define and make us. Our character and destinies

identity and guide our conduct in a rapidly evolving business environment. Spirit of Wipro Five Habits These values are our bedrock. They define and make us. Our character and destinies are energized by our values. When our behaviors and ways of working consistently reflect our values, we see Five Habits in action. Be passionate about clients' success Treat each person with respect Be global and responsible Being Respectful Being Responsive Always Communicating Demonstrating Stewardship Unyielding integrity in everything we do By aligning our governance practices with these Five Habits, our Company is fostering a high-performance culture that embraces inclusion, accountability, and transparent leadership. These behaviors ensure that our decisions are compliant and ethical while also prioritizing a human-centric and futureready approach. We consistently engage with associates to understand their experience of these habits as part of our culture conversations. More than 223,000 associates worldwide have been part of the interactive Five Habits sessions with leaders, including 38,000 managers who have been part of the sessions led by our Chairman, Mr. Rishad A. Premji. The Spirit of Wipro and the Five

Five Habits sessions with leaders, including 38,000 managers who have been part of the sessions led by our Chairman, Mr. Rishad A. Premji. The Spirit of Wipro and the Five Habits have become deeply ingrained in our daily ways of working, governance, and business conduct. Building Trust We firmly believe that effective governance starts with leadership. At Wipro, our leaders are expected to exemplify our cultural and ethical standards, champion behaviors aligned with the Five Habits, and foster an environment where individuals thrive through mutual respect, open communication, and shared accountability. This is central to our culture and guides how we lead, make decisions, and engage with stakeholders.  Wipro’s corporate governance is implemented through a multi-layered framework encompassing governance by shareholders, the Board of Directors, Board committees and management processes. This is supported by robust board governance practices, strong internal control systems and rigorous audit mechanisms that ensure transparency and accountability at all levels. These are articulated through Wipro’s Code of Business Conduct, Corporate Governance Guidelines and various board-chartered Integrated Annual Report 2024-25

REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 148 Corporate Governance Report policies. Together, these mechanisms not only ensure regulatory compliance but also reinforce the Spirit of Wipro and our Five Habits as cornerstones of ethical and performance-driven governance. In this report, we have provided details on how the corporate governance principles are put into practice within Wipro. II. SHAREHOLDERS The Companies Act, 2013, Listing Regulations and NYSE Listed Company Manual prescribes the governance mechanism by shareholders in terms of passing of ordinary and special resolutions, voting rights, participation in corporate actions such as bonus issue, buyback of shares, declaration of dividend, etc. Your Company follows a robust process to ensure that the shareholders of the Company are well informed of the Board decisions both on financial and non-financial matters and adequate notice with a detailed explanation is sent to the shareholders well in advance to obtain necessary approvals. The Company seeks approval of shareholders on various resolutions at the Annual General Meeting held every year. In addition, approval of shareholders is also sought through postal ballot in

approvals. The Company seeks approval of shareholders on various resolutions at the Annual General Meeting held every year. In addition, approval of shareholders is also sought through postal ballot in case of urgency of the matter as per the applicable regulations. are foreign nationals. The detailed profiles of all the Directors, their experience, expertise and full-time positions are available on our website at https://www. wipro.com/leadership. Board Meetings The Board meeting dates are decided in consultation with the Board members. The schedule of the Board and Committee meetings are communicated in advance to the Directors to enable them to attend the meetings. In instances of special and urgent business requirements, the Board’s approval is obtained by way of resolutions passed through circulation, in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013. Such resolutions are subsequently placed before the Board in the next meeting for noting and confirmation. The Board meetings are normally scheduled over two days. VI. GOVERNANCE THROUGH MANAGEMENT PROCESS Code of Business Conduct In the year 1983, we articulated ‘Wipro Beliefs’ consisting of six statements. At the core of beliefs was integrity, articulated as “individual and company relationship should be governed by the highest standard of conduct and integrity”. Over the years, this articulation has evolved in form but remained constant in substance. Today, we articulate it as Code of Business Conduct. In your Company, the Board and all employees have a responsibility

articulation has evolved in form but remained constant in substance. Today, we articulate it as Code of Business Conduct. In your Company, the Board and all employees have a responsibility to understand and follow the Code of Business Conduct. All employees are expected to perform their work with honesty and integrity. Wipro’s Code of Business Conduct reflects general principles to guide employees in making ethical decisions. This Code is also applicable to our representatives. This Code outlines fundamental ethical considerations as well as specific considerations that need to be maintained for professional conduct. This Code has been displayed on the Company’s website at https://www.wipro.com/content/dam/ nexus/en/investor/corporate-governance/policiesand-guidelines/ethical-guidelines/code-of-businessconduct-and-ethics.pdf. Internal Audit The Company has a robust internal audit function which has been in place for last 4 decades with the stated vision of “to be the best-in-class Internal Audit function globally”. In pursuit of this vision, the function provides an independent, objective assurance services to value-add and improve operations of Business Units and processes by: a) Financial, Business Process and Compliance Audit b) Cyber Defense and Technology Audit c) Operations Reviews d) Best practices

to value-add and improve operations of Business Units and processes by: a) Financial, Business Process and Compliance Audit b) Cyber Defense and Technology Audit c) Operations Reviews d) Best practices and benchmarking e) Anti-Fraud reviews including Anti-Bribery, AntiCorruption compliances, Anti-Money Laundering Compliances etc. Integrated Annual Report 2024-25

REPORTING CONTEXT CORPORATE OVERVIEW PERFORMANCE AND OUTLOOK SUSTAINABILITY AT WIPRO 160 Corporate Governance Report The function taking cognizance of changes in business climate and technology risks has taken upon itself to infuse and adopt technology in its operations. Internal Audit function applies analytical methods to derive a higher degree of assurance on the audit areas and to pro-actively identify risk events. The Head of Internal Audit reports to the Chairman of the Audit, Risk and Compliance Committee and administratively to the Chief Risk & Assurance Officer. The Head of Internal Audit has regular and exclusive meetings with the Audit, Risk and Compliance Committee. The internal audit function is guided by its charter, as approved by the Audit, Risk and Compliance Committee. The internal audit function formulates an annual risk based audit plan based on consultations and inputs from the Board and business leaders and presents it to the Audit, Risk and Compliance Committee for approval. Findings of various audits carried out during the financial year are also periodically presented to the Audit, Risk and Compliance Committee. The internal audit function

and Compliance Committee for approval. Findings of various audits carried out during the financial year are also periodically presented to the Audit, Risk and Compliance Committee. The internal audit function adopts a risk based audit approach and covers core areas such as compliance audits, financial audits, technology audits, third party risk audits, M&A audits, etc. The internal audit team comprises of personnel with professional qualifications and certifications in audit and is rich on diversity. The audit team hones its skills through a robust knowledge management program to continuously assimilate the latest trends and skills in the domain and to retain the knowledge gained for future reference and dissemination. The internal audit team re-asserts its independence across all its staff. A key strategic vision of Internal Audit is auditing in the new digital environment - in line with this, the Internal Audit function has actively adopted Technology and Innovation to be better equipped to carry out audits. The function, which was the first Indian Internal audit unit to get ISO certified in 1998 and win International award from Institute of Internal

better equipped to carry out audits. The function, which was the first Indian Internal audit unit to get ISO certified in 1998 and win International award from Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA) in 2002, was also an early adopter of the new ISO 9001:2015 Version. ISO certification is annually renewed/ retained by a review by external accreditation body (DNV). Internal Audit function is in conformance to the International Standards as prescribed by the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing issued by International Institute of Internal Auditors (“IIA”). The conformance has been reviewed by external firm (KPMG) since financial year 2019-20. the shareholders for a period of seven consecutive years, were transferred to the IEPF Authority. In case of non-receipt of response to the reminders from the shareholders, the unclaimed shares are transferred to the Unclaimed Suspense Account. The Company maintains the details of shareholding of each individual shareholders whose shares are transferred to the Unclaimed Suspense Account. When a claim from a shareholder is received by the Company, the shares lying in the Unclaimed Suspense Account are transferred after due verification of documents submitted by the shareholder. Further, the shares in respect of which dividend entitlements remained unclaimed for seven consecutive years are transferred from the Unclaimed Suspense Account to IEPF Authority in accordance with Section 124(6) of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder. SEBI, vide its circular dated January 25, 2022, mandated that the Company/

Account to IEPF Authority in accordance with Section 124(6) of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder. SEBI, vide its circular dated January 25, 2022, mandated that the Company/ RTA shall verify and process the investor service requests and thereafter issue a ‘Letter of Confirmation (“LOC”)’ in lieu of physical share certificate(s). The LOC shall be valid for a period of one hundred twenty days from the date of issuance within which the Member/Claimant shall make a request to the Depository Participant for dematerialising the said shares. In case, the demat request is not submitted within the aforesaid period, the shares shall be credited to the Company’s Suspense Escrow Demat Account. As on March 31, 2025, there are no shares lying in the Company’s Suspense Escrow Demat Account.

Act, 2013. Shareholders holding equity shares as on the cut-off date may cast their votes through remote e-voting process during the voting period fixed for this purpose.
